coeur.label: heart
coeur.desc: Taxon notes:" the ascidian tube-like heart lacks chambers....The ascidian heart is formed after metamorphosis as a simple tube-like structure with a single-layered myoepithelium that is continuous with a single-layered pericar- dial wall. It lacks chambers and endocardium.... The innovation of the chambered heart was a key event in vertebrate evolution, because the chambered heart generates one-wa

parents.label: structure with developmental contribution from neural crest

ventricule_g.label: heart left ventricle
ventricule_g.desc: A cardiac ventricle that is in the left side of the heart.

ventricule_d.label: heart right ventricle
ventricule_d.desc: A cardiac ventricle that is in the right side of the heart.

oreillettes.label: cardiac atrium
oreillettes.desc: Cardiac chamber in which blood enters the heart.

valves.label: cardiac valve
valves.desc: A membranous fold of the heart that prevents reflux of fluid.

aorte.label: aorta
aorte.desc: The main trunk of the systemic arterial system that carries blood from the heart to all the organs and other structures of the body, bringing oxygenated blood to all parts of the body in the systemic circulation.

coronaires.label: coronary artery
coronaires.desc: An artery that supplies the myocardium.

carotides.label: internal carotid artery
carotides.desc: A terminal branch of the left or right common carotid artery which supplies oxygenated blood to the brain and eyes[MP].

art_pulmonaire.label: pulmonary artery
art_pulmonaire.desc: An artery that carries deoxygenated blood from heart to the lungs. They are the only arteries (other than umbilical arteries in the fetus) that carry deoxygenated blood..

pericarde.label: pericardium
pericarde.desc: The combination of pericardial sac (a double-walled sac containing the heart and the roots of the great vessels) plus fibrous pericardium.

poumons.label: lung
poumons.desc: Snakes and limbless lizards typically possess only the right lung as a major respiratory organ; the left lung is greatly reduced, or even absent. Amphisbaenians, however, have the opposite arrangement, with a major left lung, and a reduced or absent right lung [WP]

poumon_g.label: left lung
poumon_g.desc: Lung which consists of the left upper lobe and left lower lobe.[FMA].

bronches.label: bronchus
bronches.desc: The upper conducting airways of the lung; these airways arise from the terminus of the trachea.

trachee.label: trachea
trachee.desc: The trachea is the portion of the airway that attaches to the bronchi as it branches [GO:dph].

plevre.label: pleura
plevre.desc: The invaginated serous membrane that surrounds the lungs (the visceral portion) and lines the walls of the pleural cavity (parietal portion).

diaphragme.label: diaphragm
diaphragme.desc: A thin musculomebranous barrier that separates the abdominal and thoracic cavities. Often used for breathing control.

larynx.label: larynx
larynx.desc: A continuation of the pharynx that is involved in breathing, sound production, and protecting the trachea against food aspiration.

foie.label: liver
foie.desc: An exocrine gland which secretes bile and functions in metabolism of protein and carbohydrate and fat, synthesizes substances involved in the clotting of the blood, synthesizes vitamin A, detoxifies poisonous substances, stores glycogen, and breaks down worn-out erythrocytes[GO].

vesicule.label: gallbladder
vesicule.desc: Rats do not have a gallbladder, but produce bile. The bile flows directly from the liver through the (hepatic) bile duct into the small intestine (Hebel and Stromberg, 1988)

pancreas.label: pancreas
pancreas.desc: An endoderm derived structure that produces precursors of digestive enzymes and blood glucose regulating hormones[GO].

estomac.label: stomach
estomac.desc: An expanded region of the vertebrate alimentary tract that serves as a food storage compartment and digestive organ. A stomach is lined, in whole or in part by a glandular epithelium.

oesophage.label: esophagus
oesophage.desc: Tube that connects the pharynx to the stomach. In mammals, the oesophagus connects the buccal cavity with the stomach. The stratified squamous non-keratinised epithelium lining the buccal cavity is continued through the pharynx down into the oesophagus. The lowest part of the oesophagus (ca. 2 cm) is lined with gastric mucosa and covered by peritoneum. The main body of the oesophagus is lined with

intestin_grele.label: small intestine
intestin_grele.desc: Subdivision of digestive tract that connects the stomach to the large intestine and is where much of the digestion and absorption of food takes place (with the exception of ruminants). The mammalian small intestine is long and coiled and can be differentiated histologically into: duodenum, jejunem, ileum[WP,cjm,Kardong].

colon.label: colon
colon.desc: A portion of the large intestine before it becomes the rectum. In mammals, the colon is the most part of the large intestine, excluding the vermiform appendix, the rectum and the anal canal.

appendice.label: vermiform appendix
appendice.desc: A blind-ended tube connected to the cecum, from which it develops embryologically[WP].

rectum.label: rectum
rectum.desc: The terminal portion of the intestinal tube, terminating with the anus.

rate.label: spleen
rate.desc: The organ that functions to filter blood and to store red corpuscles and platelets.

peritoine.label: peritoneum
peritoine.desc: A serous membrane that lines the peritoneal cavity[VHOG,modified].

reins.label: kidney
reins.desc: A paired organ of the urinary tract that produces urine and maintains bodily fluid homeostasis, blood pressure, pH levels, red blood cell production and skeleton mineralization.

uretere.label: ureter
uretere.desc: Muscular duct that propels urine from the kidneys to the urinary bladder, or related organs.

vessie.label: urinary bladder
vessie.desc: Distensible musculomembranous organ situated in the anterior part of the pelvic cavity in which urine collects before excretion[MP].

urethre.label: urethra
urethre.desc: The fibromuscular tubular canal through which urine is discharged from the bladder to the exterior via the external urinary meatus; in males, the urethra is joined by the ejaculatory ducts and serves as a passageway for semen during ejaculation, as well as a canal for urine during voiding; in females, the urethra is shorter and emerges above the vaginal opening.

prostate.label: prostate gland
prostate.desc: The prostate gland is a partly muscular, partly glandular body that is situated near the base of the mammalian male urethra and secretes an alkaline viscid fluid which is a major constituent of the ejaculatory fluid.

cerveau.label: brain
cerveau.desc: The brain is the center of the nervous system in all vertebrate, and most invertebrate, animals. Some primitive animals such as jellyfish and starfish have a decentralized nervous system without a brain, while sponges lack any nervous system at all. In vertebrates, the brain is located in the head, protected by the skull and close to the primary sensory apparatus of vision, hearing, balance, taste

cervelet.label: cerebellum
cervelet.desc: Part of the metencephalon that lies in the posterior cranial fossa behind the brain stem. It is concerned with the coordination of movement[MESH]. A large dorsally projecting part of the brain concerned especially with the coordination of muscles and the maintenance of bodily equilibrium, situated between the brain stem and the back of the cerebrum , and formed in humans of two lateral lobes and a

tronc_cerebral.label: brainstem
tronc_cerebral.desc: 'brainstem' is a loose term that sometimes refers to the ventral parts o the brain except for any part of the telencephalon - sometimes it includes the diencephalon or subpallial telencephalon structures (ISBN:0471888893). Here we use it in a more restriced sense, to include only the medulla oblongata, pons (when present) and the midbrain tegmentum (following the ZFA definitions).

moelle.label: spinal cord
moelle.desc: Part of the central nervous system located in the vertebral canal continuous with and caudal to the brain; demarcated from brain by plane of foramen magnum. It is composed of an inner core of gray matter in which nerve cells predominate, and an outer layer of white matter in which myelinated nerve fibers predominate, and surrounds the central canal. (CUMBO).

parents.label: structure with developmental contribution from neural crest

meninges.label: meninx
meninges.desc: Membrane organ that surrounds the brain and the spinal cord.

hippocampe.label: hippocampal formation
hippocampe.desc: Hippocampus (proper) plus dentate gyrus and subiculum[definition derived from NIF comments and ontology alignment].

noyaux_gris.label: basal ganglion
noyaux_gris.desc: An individual member of a collection of basal ganglia. Basal ganglia are subcortical masses of gray matter in the forebrain and midbrain that are richly interconnected and so viewed as a functional system. The nuclei usually included are the caudate nucleus (caudoputamen in rodents), putamen, globus pallidus, substantia nigra (pars compacta and pars reticulata) and the subthalamic nucleus. Some al

nerfs_craniens.label: cranial nerve
nerfs_craniens.desc: Cranial nerves are nerves that emerge directly from the brain, in contrast to spinal nerves, which emerge from segments of the spinal cord.

children.label: chorda tympani branch of facial nerve

nerfs_periph.label: nerve
nerfs_periph.desc: An enclosed, cable-like bundle of axons in the peripheral nervous system originating in a nerve root in the central nervous system (or a condensed nervous structure) connecting with peripheral structures.

oreille.label: ear
oreille.desc: Sense organ in vertebrates that is specialized for the detection of sound, and the maintenance of balance. Includes the outer ear and middle ear, which collect and transmit sound waves; and the inner ear, which contains the organs of balance and (except in fish) hearing. Also includes the pinna, the visible part of the outer ear, present in some mammals.

parents.label: structure with developmental contribution from neural crest

thyroide.label: thyroid gland
thyroide.desc: A two-lobed endocrine gland found in all vertebrates, located in front of and on either side of the trachea in humans, and producing various hormones, such as triiodothyronine and calcitonin[BTO].

parents.label: structure with developmental contribution from neural crest

parathyroides.label: parathyroid gland
parathyroides.desc: The parathyroid gland is an endocrine gland for secretion of parathyroid hormone, usually found as a pair, embedded in the connective tissue capsule on the posterior surface of the thyroid gland. Parathyroid regulates calcium and phosphorous metabolism.

surrenales.label: adrenal gland
surrenales.desc: Either of a pair of complex endocrine organs near the anterior medial border of the kidney consisting of a mesodermal cortex that produces glucocorticoid, mineralocorticoid, and androgenic hormones and an ectodermal medulla that produces epinephrine and norepinephrine[BTO].

parents.label: structure with developmental contribution from neural crest

hypophyse.label: pituitary gland
hypophyse.desc: Terminology note: hypophysis and pituitary gland are often used informally as synonyms, but the hypophysis also includes the small infundibular or pituitary stalk[Neumann 2020]

hypothalamus.label: hypothalamus
hypothalamus.desc: A specialized brain region of the ventral diencephalon arising near the end of the segmentation period; the embryonic hypothalamic region will give rise to the posterior pituitary gland as well as a number of brain nuclei. [ZFA]. One of the most important functions of the hypothalamus is to link the nervous system to the endocrine system via the pituitary gland (hypophysis).[Wikipedia].

thymus.label: thymus
thymus.desc: Anatomical structure of largely lymphoid tissue that functions in cell-mediated immunity by being the site where T cells develop.

uterus.label: uterus
uterus.desc: The female muscular organ of gestation in which the developing embryo or fetus is nourished until birth.

ovaires.label: ovary
ovaires.desc: The gonad of a female organism which contains germ cells.
syn: gonad of female organism reproductive system
gonad of genitalia of female organism
gonad of reproductive system of female organism

trompes.label: fallopian tube
trompes.desc: Initial section of the oviduct through which the ova pass from the ovary to the uterus.

placenta.label: placenta
placenta.desc: Marsupials possess only a rudimentary yolk-type placenta, with reduced nutrient and oxygen exchanging capabilities.

vagin.label: vagina
vagin.desc: A fibromuscular tubular tract leading from the uterus to the exterior of the body in female placental mammals and marsupials, or to the cloaca in female birds, monotremes, and some reptiles[WP].

testicules.label: testis
testicules.desc: A gonad of a male animal. A gonad produces and releases sperm.

ganglions.label: lymph node
ganglions.desc: Any of the rounded masses of lymphoid tissue that are surrounded by a capsule of connective tissue, are distributed along the lymphatic vessels, and contain numerous lymphocytes which filter the flow of lymph.

moelle_osseuse.label: bone marrow
moelle_osseuse.desc: The soft tissue that fills the cavities of bones.

rachis.label: vertebral column
rachis.desc: Subdivision of skeletal system that consists of all the vertebra and associated skeletal elements and joints in the body[modified from VSAO].

crane.label: cranium
crane.desc: Upper portion of the skull that excludes the mandible (when present in the organism).

bassin.label: bony pelvis
bassin.desc: A ring of bone formed by the sacrum and the first few coccygeal vertebrae as the roof, the pubis and ischia as the floor and the ilia and the acetabular part of the ischia as the walls.

os_mi.label: long bone
os_mi.desc: Endochondral_ossification is an essential process during the rudimentary formation of long bones, with the exception of the clavicle[Wikipedia:Endochondral_ossification]. The medial and and lateral ends undergo EO, the mid-portion is formed by a process with features of EO & IO (the process is shared by the mandible)[ISBN:9780397517251]

muscles_mi.label: muscle of leg
muscles_mi.desc: A muscle that is part of the region of the hindlimb between the pelvis and ankle.
syn: muscle of hindlimb zeugopod or stylopod
muscle of upper or lower hindlimb segment

peau.label: skin of body
peau.desc: The organ covering the body that consists of the dermis and epidermis.

parents.label: structure with developmental contribution from neural crest

tissu_adipeux.label: adipose tissue
tissu_adipeux.desc: Portion of connective tissue composed of adipocytes enmeshed in areolar tissue.

sein.label: breast
sein.desc: The upper ventral region of the torso of an organism.

organs_removed.reason: UBERON:0001497 = muscle de la ceinture pelvienne

_meta.removal_note: 6 entrees retirees : identifiants UBERON incorrects (regroupements de commodite de l'atlas sans terme UBERON unique). A re-verifier manuellement sur ebi.ac.uk/ols4 avant tout reajout.
